description.value:
You flood the area with a wave of electrical interference. You deal 7d8 electricity damage to creatures in the area. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 1 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 2 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You launch a torrent of magical nanites that begin dissolving your target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, you deal 3d8 acid damage plus 1d6 persistent acid damage. On a critical hit, double the initial damage, but not the persistent damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Impersonate] the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Treat Wounds] on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Recall Knowledge] about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Spell Effect: Analyze Target]

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You manifest an injury from the future or past to harm a creature in the present. Choose bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. The target takes 1d8 of the selected damage type with a basic Will save. If the target fails its save and then takes the same type of damage before the end of your next turn, it takes an additional @Damage[ceil((@item.level)/2)d4[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a failure increases by 1d4.

You touch a weapon with no remaining ammunition and recharge it with magical energy. The target gains ammunition or charges equal to its expend until the end of your next turn, allowing anyone holding the target to make one Strike with the weapon. This spell only recharges weapons that use ammunition with a cost of 10 credits or less.

Heightened (3rd) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to double its expend.

Heightened (6th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to triple its expend.

Heightened (9th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to quadruple its expend.

You draw in and condense ambient magical energy from around you to power up a weapon. The next Strike made using that weapon is supercharged. That Strike deals an additional 1d6 damage of the same type as the weapon's normal damage.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Spell Effect: Supercharge Weapon]

Heightened (+2) The extra damage increases by 1d6.

target.value: 1 weapon held by you or a willing ally

description.value:
Upgrades: 0

A mainstay of the Azlanti Star Empire, this sleek laser rifle includes a slot for aeon stones to enhance and focus the intensity of its beams.

This circuit safely courses electricity through your weapon. It deals an extra 1d6 electricity damage. On a critical hit, electricity arcs out to deal an equal amount of electricity damage to up to two other creatures of your choice within 10 feet of the target.

Upgrades: 1

These durable gloves fit over a wielder's hands (or other appendages) and feature weighted plates or spikes over the knuckles.

Upgrades: 0

These decorative suits of medium armor feature exaggerated shoulder guards, stylized dragon wings, and draconic emblems across the breastplate's back and chest. Skyfire armor is often plated with gold or other attractive metals, sacrificing upgrade space for style.
This visor wraps around your visual organs, enhancing your natural senses. You gain darkvision.

Staple items within the Azlanti Star Empire, these gemstones are popular status symbols for adventurers and wealthy collectors. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to build the Universe in ancient times.

When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head (or another visible part of your anatomy if you don't have a head) instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an aeon stone with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Disarm] action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Suppressed] until you return it to orbit your head again.

There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into specific items, such as weapons with the aeon trait or antique magical devices called wayfinders.

This nacreous, tapered cylinder provides a breath of air when you need it most. You can hold your breath for twice as long, and you gain a +1 item bonus to saves against inhaled poisons. The resonant power allows you to cast @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.Life Seal] as a divine innate spell once per day.

Trigger The Sihedron commander is reduced to 0 Hit Points and has used their Reward of Obedience reaction in the last year. The Sihedron commander must use this ability when the trigger is met

Effect The Sihedron commander is utterly destroyed, leaving behind only their equipment. Their soul can't be restored, and no magical means can contact their soul.

Frequency once per year

Trigger The Sihedron commander is reduced to 0 Hit Points but not from an effect that leaves no remains (like promession)

Effect The Sihedron commander restores @Damage[6d6[healing]] Hit Points and doesn't fall @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Prone] or drop their equipment.

Frequency once per day

Effect The Sihedron commander gains a fly Speed for 1 minute equal to their Speed.

The Sihedron commander attempts a ranged Strike with their aeon rifle or a melee Strike with their battleglove, then Casts a Spell requiring 2 actions or fewer against the same target. If the spell requires an attack roll, don't count the ranged Strike toward their multiple attack penalty.
